name: TAO 4h Failed Breakdown Hold v1
edge_mechanism: a breakdown attempt fails, price reclaims the broken support, holds above it, and resumes with the higher-volatility trend context rather than mean reverting all the way to balance
thesis: The cycle explicitly wants failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry. TAO 4h is the best-fit structurally different lane because it already carries live trend-up contamination. That makes failed breakdowns more likely to become trend resumptions than pure balance rotations, which gives this concept a different job than the BTC reclaim mean reversion variant.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h failed breakdowns do not hold above reclaimed structure long enough to produce continuation, or the setup stays under screen density, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below local support, rejects the move, reclaims support, holds it, and then resumes in the dominant direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In an asset with live trend-up contamination, failed breakdowns can trap late shorts and fuel a stronger continuation than ordinary range reclaims. The hold requirement is there to separate actual rejection from a one-bar bounce.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because the same failed-breakdown grammar has more room to trend-resume there than in flat ETH 4h, while still staying inside the allowed basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: TAO may be too extended, turning reclaim entries into late continuation buys right before a deeper pullback.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the entry works but drawdown is too large, add faster partial-profit extraction before reducing the hold requirement.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than BTC 1h, but better asymmetry when the reclaim genuinely restarts the trend leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears screen density and the reclaim-hold grammar produces at least one lane with acceptable post-cost continuation behavior.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated reclaim-hold entries that fail back through support within 1-2 bars.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because the hold requirement is simple and the asset has larger swings

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for failed breakdowns that can resolve into trend resumption.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h produces enough reclaim-hold resumptions with positive first-target behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h reclaim-hold entries repeatedly fail back through structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a few strong continuation runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same failed-breakdown hold works in a less directional major.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves the reclaim-hold behavior even if the move resolves more modestly.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h turns it into weak range noise rather than continuation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller but more frequent reclaim-hold continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful negative control to see whether the thesis depends on trend contamination.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows orderly reclaim-hold participation.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h proves the strategy is too dependent on TAO's trend structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude and more modest continuation than TAO.

long: Next bar low remains >= DONCHIAN_LOW_20 - 0.12 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high remains <= DONCHIAN_HIGH_20 + 0.12 * ATR_14
